label: Pinecone
description: Query and upsert vectors in a Pinecone managed vector database for semantic search and AI-powered retrieval.

form.title: Connect Pinecone
form.subtitle: Provide your API key, environment, and index name to connect Anton to your Pinecone vector database.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste your Pinecone API key along with your environment and index name. The fastest way to get connected — no OAuth flow required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Pinecone API Key

### Get your API key

1. Log in to [Pinecone Console](https://app.pinecone.io).
2. In the left sidebar, click **API Keys**.
3. Click **Create API Key**, give it a name like `Anton`, and click **Create**.
4. Copy the key — it is shown once. Store it somewhere safe.

### Find your environment

1. In the Pinecone Console, look at the top-right corner or open **Indexes**.
2. Each index shows its **Environment** (e.g. `us-west1-gcp`, `us-east1-aws`, `eu-west1-gcp`). Copy it exactly — it must match.

### Find your index name

1. Open **Indexes** in the left sidebar.
2. Copy the exact name of the index Anton should read from and write to.

### Notes

- API keys are scoped to your Pinecone project. If you have multiple projects, make sure the key, environment, and index all belong to the same project.
- If you rotate or delete the key in the Pinecone Console, return here and update it.
- Pinecone's newer Serverless indexes (introduced in 2024) don't use a regional environment string — leave the environment field as `us-east-1` or the region shown in the Console for your serverless index.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Pinecone Console → API Keys. Shown only once at creation — copy it before closing the dialog.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: The cloud region your index is hosted in, e.g. us-west1-gcp or us-east1-aws. Visible on the Indexes page next to each index name.

fields.name: index_name
fields.label: Index Name
fields.description: The exact name of the Pinecone index Anton should connect to.

fields.name: namespace
fields.label: Namespace
fields.description: Optional namespace within the index to scope reads and writes. Leave blank to use the default namespace.
